I said to 'Ubaidullah Al-Khawlani: 'Didn't Zaid tell us about images yesterday?' 'Ubaidullah said: 'Didn't you hear him say: Except for patterns on fabrics?'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated from Abu Talhah that:

translation.text: "I made some food and invited the Prophet  (to come and eat). He came and entered, then he saw a curtain on which there were images, so he went out and said: 'The Angels do not enter a house in which there are images.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Ali said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  went out, then he came in, and I had hung up a curtain on which there were (images of) horses with wings. When he saw it, he said: 'Get rid of it.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah said:

translation.text: "We had a curtain on which there were images of birds, at the entrance to the house. The Messenger of Allah  said: 'O 'Aishah, remove it, for ever time I come in and see it, I remember this world.'" She said: "We had a plush wrap, with a border on it, that we would wear, and it was not cut off."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah, the wife of the Prophet , said:

translation.text: "In my house there was a cloth on which were images, which I put in a niche of the house, and the Messenger of Allah  used to pray facing it. Then he said: 'O 'Aishah, take it away from me.' So I took it down and made it into pillows.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah said:

translation.text: She put up a curtain on which there were images, then the Messenger of Allah  used to recline on them.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated from 'Aishah that:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  came from a journey, and I had hung a curtain on which there were images over a niche. He took it down and said: 'The people who will be most severely punished on the Day of Resurrection will be those who try to match the creation of Allah.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  entered upon me, and I had put up a curtain on which there were images. When he saw it, his face changed color, then he tore it down with his hand and said: 'The people who will be most severely punished on the Day of Resurrection will be those who try to imitate the creation of Allah.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah, the wife of the Prophet , said:

translation.text: "I was sitting with Ibn 'Abbas when a man from among the people of Al-'Iraq came to him and said: 'I make these images; what do you say concerning them?' He said: 'Come closer, come closer. I heard Muhammad  say: Whoever makes an image in this world will be commanded on the Day of Resurrection to breathe a soul into it, and he will not be able to do so."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that An-Nadr bin Anas said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  said: 'Whoever makes an image will be punished until (he is commanded) to breathe a soul into it, and he will not be able to do so.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Ibn 'Abbas said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  said: 'Whoever makes an image will be commanded on the Day of Resurrection to breathe the soul into it but he will not be able to do so.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Abu Hurairah said:

translation.text: The Prophet  said: "The makers of these images will be punished on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them: 'Breathe life into that which you have created.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated from Ibn 'Umar that:

translation.text: The Messenger of Allah  said: "The makers of these images will be punished on the Day of Resurrection, and it will be said to them: 'Bring to life that which you have created.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated from 'Aishah, the wife of the Prophet , that:

translation.text: "The people who will be most severely punished on the Day of Resurrection will be those who try to match the creation of Allah."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah, the wife of the Prophet , said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  said: 'Among the people who will be most severely punished on the Day of Resurrection will be the image-makers.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Abdullah said:

translation.text: "Jibril, peace be upon him, asked permission to enter upon the Prophet  and he said: 'Come in.' He said: 'How can I come in when there is a curtain in your house on which there are images? You should either cut off their heads or make it into a rug to be stepped on, for we Angels do not enter a house in which there are images.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Abu Hurairah said:

translation.text: "The Messenger of Allah  would not pray in our blankets."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Aishah said:

translation.text: The sandals of the Messenger of Allah  had two straps.
translation.narrator: Anas narrated that :

translation.text: "The sandals of the Messenger of Allah  had two straps."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Amr bin Aws said:

translation.text: The Prophet  said: "If the strap of the sandal of one of you breaks, let him not walk in one sandal until he fixes it."
translation.narrator: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah that:

translation.text: "I saw Abu Hurairah clap his hand to his forehead and say: 'O people of Al-'Iraq, you claim that I tell lies about the Messenger of Allah  say: If the strap of the sandal of one of you breaks, let him not walk in the other until he fixes it.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Abu Razin said:

translation.text: The Prophet  smiled.
translation.narrator: It was narrated from Anas bin Malik that:

translation.text: "I came to Abu Hashim bin 'Utbah when he was suffering the plague, and Mu'awiyah came to visit him. Abu Hashim wept. Mu'awiyah said to him: 'Why are you weeping? Is it because of some pain that is hurting you, or is it for this world, the best of which has gone?' He said: 'Neither, but the Messenger of Allah  gave me some advice, which I wish that I had followed. He said: "Perhaps you will live to see wealth that will be distributed among the people when all that would suffice you of that would be a servant and a mount to ride in the cause of Allah." I lived to see that, and I accumulated (wealth).'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Samurah bin Sahm said:

translation.text: "The pommel of the sword of the Messenger of Allah  was of silver."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Abu Umamah bin Sahl said:

translation.text: "The metallic end of the scabbard of the Messenger of Allah  was of silver, the pommel of his sword was silver, and in between were rings of silver."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Anas said:

translation.text: "The pommel of the sword of the Messenger of Allah  was of silver."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Sa'eed bin Abi Al-Hasan said:

translation.text: The Messenger of Allah  said to me: "Say: O Allah, make me steadfast and guide me." And he forbade me to sit on Al-Mayathir." Al-Mayathir: Qassi which the women used to put on the saddles for their husbands, such as red cushions.
translation.narrator: It was narrated that 'Ali said:

translation.text: "Abu Rifa'ah said: 'I came to the Messenger of Allah  sat down on it and started to teach me what Allah has taught him, then he went and completed his Khutbah.'"
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Humaid bin Hilal said:

translation.text: "We were with the Prophet (ﷺ) in Al-Batha' and he was in a red tent, and some people were with him, and he was about to set out. Bilal came and called the Adhan, turning this way and that."
translation.narrator: It was narrated that Abu Juhaifah said:
